operating_state: Consolidated rental revenues increased to $5.1 billion for nine months driven by Duke Transaction acquisition and strong rent growth. Rent change on rollover reached 77.2% reflecting market expansion and 62% lease mark-to-market opportunity. Same store NOI growth of 9.3% with 97.5% occupancy maintained across portfolio. Operating distributions from unconsolidated entities increased to $536 million from $316 million.
cost_structure: Real Estate NOI expanded to $3.8 billion from $2.5 billion with rental expenses increasing proportionally due to Duke integration and higher insurance costs. G&A expenses rose to $292 million driven by equity compensation increases and inflationary pressures. Interest payments increased to $340 million from $189 million reflecting higher debt levels. Depreciation increased $646 million primarily from Duke acquisition asset base expansion.
material_events: Completed $3.1 billion acquisition of 70 properties totaling 14 million square feet with $28 million transaction costs. Generated $1.4 billion net proceeds from property contributions and sales realizing $432 million gains. Issued $5.3 billion senior notes and $312 million term loans at 4.7% weighted average rate while making $270 million debt payments. Formed Japan Core Logistics Fund and earned $674 million promote revenues primarily from USLF.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency exposure across pound sterling, euro, yen, and Canadian dollar with natural hedging through local borrowings and derivatives. Interest rate risk increased with $5.4 billion new debt at higher rates. Supply chain disruption risks persist though demand fundamentals support rent growth.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued organic NOI growth from 62% lease mark-to-market opportunity and market rent expansion. Development pipeline of $6.9 billion with $2.5 billion remaining investment provides value creation. Strategic Capital positioned for growth through expanded co-investment platform. Available liquidity of $6.9 billion supports acquisition and development activity.
uncertainty_profile: Development completion risk with 110 properties requiring $2.5 billion additional investment through 2025. REIT status requires meeting dividend distribution requirements with potential taxes on undistributed income. Co-investment venture commitments of $894 million require fulfillment. Unconsolidated ventures carry $14.3 billion third-party debt at 27.6% loan-to-value though company provides no guarantees.
